title: site_sets.tsv row — WHICH variant rows
description:
One set of variant sites (e.g. SNPs after MAF filter and 500-bp thinning). FK chain: site_set → parent_site_set + interval_set + coordinate_system.

IMPORTANT: BEAGLE genotype-likelihood files are NOT site sets — they're INPUT VALUES (input_values.tsv). A site set lists rows; an input_value carries the per-(sample × site) values for those rows.

path.description: Path to the sites TSV(.gz). Typical columns: chrom, pos, ref, alt.

parent_site_set_id.description: Optional FK: the bigger set this was thinned/intersected from.

interval_set_id.description: Optional FK: the coordinate scope (genome / chromosome / candidate).

operation.description: How this set was derived from parent (e.g. 'thin', 'intersect', 'maf_filter').

params_id.description: FK or tag identifying the operation parameters (e.g. 'thin500_first_per_chrom_v1').
